title: Top 7 Herbal Creams to Alleviate Red Eyes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for red eyes because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and calm irritated blood vessels in the eyes, providing relief from redness and discomfort.

Kaempferol is good for red eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the blood vessels, thus easing the redness and discomfort associated with irritated or inflamed eyes.
Tannins are good for red eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated blood vessels, which can cause redness and discomfort in the eyes.
Carotenoids are good for red eyes because they help to reduce inflammation and repair damaged cells in the eyes, which can help to alleviate redness and irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams are a popular remedy for red eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The main active compound in calendula, triterpenoids, helps to reduce swelling and redness by limiting the flow of blood to the affected area. This results in a decrease in the visibility of the redness, providing relief to the individual. Additionally, calendula creams have antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infections and promote healing in the eye area. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort. This makes calendula creams an effective natural remedy for red eyes, especially those caused by allergies, irritants, or minor eye infections. Regular use of calendula creams may also help to strengthen the skin's natural barrier, reducing the frequency and severity of red eye episodes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal cream that helps with red eyes, start by drying the St. John's Wort flowers and leaves in a warm, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Once dry, grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Mix 1 part of the St. John's Wort powder with 3 parts of a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 30-60 seconds, stirring occasionally, until the powder is fully dissolved. Remove the heat and let the mixture cool. Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Add 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax to the oil mixture and heat it gently in the double boiler or microwave until the beeswax is fully melted. Remove the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken. Once set, your Hypericum perforatum herbal cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area to help soothe and reduce redness.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for red eyes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones may help alleviate red eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and soothe irritated blood vessels, thereby providing relief from redness and discomfort.
Flavonoids are good for red eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated blood vessels in the eyes.
Tannins are good for red eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and calm irritated blood vessels in the eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is a great remedy for red eyes. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. When we get red eyes, it's often because our eyes are irritated or inflamed, and the cream helps to calm this irritation. Aloe barbadensis cream also has soothing properties that help to cool and calm the skin around the eyes, making it feel more comfortable and relaxed. Additionally, the cream can help to reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes, giving you a more refreshed and awake look. The cream is also easy to apply and gentle on the skin, making it a great option for people who are sensitive to other eye creams.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal creams that help with red eyes, first gather the necessary ingredients and equipment. You will need dried Hypericum perforatum flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Start by making a herbal infusion by mixing 1 tablespoon of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers with 4 tablespoons of carrier oil in a small bowl. Heat the mixture over the double boiler or in the heat-proof bowl, gently warming it for about 10-15 minutes or until the mixture has cooled to room temperature. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the dried flowers, and discard the solids. Next, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a separate double boiler or heat-proof bowl, and mix it with the herbal infusion. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved, then remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency similar to thin honey. Pour the mixture into a clean jar or tin, and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the cream, gently apply a thin layer to the affected area around your eyes, and massage it in until it is fully absorbed.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for red eyes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for red eyes because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease irritation, allowing the eyes to heal and return to their normal color.
Kaempferol is good for red eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the eyes.
Tannins, also known as St. John's Wort, can help reduce red eyes by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent and astringent, thereby reducing swelling and excess fluid in the eyes.
Rutin helps reduce red eyes by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation, making it a useful remedy for soothing irritated and bloodshot eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis cream is a popular treatment for red eyes. This is because the cream contains an extract from the Euphrasia plant, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When applied to the eyes, it helps to reduce swelling and ease irritation. The cream also has antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infections that can cause redness and discomfort. Additionally, Euphrasia officinalis cream is gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for people with sensitive eyes. It's also easy to apply and can be used as often as needed. The cream can be used to treat a range of red eye symptoms, including conjunctivitis, dry eyes, and allergies. It can help to calm the eyes and provide relief from redness and discomfort. The cream is also often used to treat eye strain and fatigue.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal cream that helps with red eyes, you will need a few simple ingredients. First, you will need to dry the Hypericum perforatum flowers, which can be done by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the flowers are completely dry, you will need to make a herbal infusion by steeping a tablespoon of dried flowers in a cup of boiling water for about 5-7 minutes. Let the mixture cool down, then strain it to remove the flowers. Next, you will need to mix the cooled infusion with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of about 1:5 (one part infusion to five parts oil). This mixture will be the base for your cream. To make the cream, you will need to heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, until the oil has melted and the mixture has reached a temperature of about 160-180°F (71-82°C). Once the mixture has cooled down to room temperature, you can add a thickening agent, such as beeswax or candelilla wax, to give the cream its desired consistency. Finally, you can add a few drops of a natural preservative, such as vitamin E oil, to help extend the shelf life of the cream. Your Hypericum perforatum herbal cream is now ready to use, and can be applied to the affected area to help soothe and reduce redness.
remedies.image_alt: Euphrasia officinalis herbal creams for red eyes

Quercetin is good for red eyes because it has powerful anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease irritation in the blood vessels of the eyes, promoting quick recovery and relief from redness.
Alkylamides, commonly known as St. John's Wort, are good for red eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritation in the blood vessels of the eyes.
Polyphenols, also known as St. John's Wort, may help soothe red eyes due to its anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and calm irritated tissues in the eye.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea cream is a natural remedy that can help with red eyes. The main cause of red eyes is usually inflammation, which can be caused by allergies, dry air, or fatigue. Echinacea purpurea contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. When applied topically as a cream, these compounds can be absorbed quickly into the skin, providing fast relief from redness and discomfort. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing the appearance of redness. Regular use of Echinacea purpurea cream can also help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy, refreshed appearance.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal creams to help with red eyes, you will need dried Hypericum perforatum flowers, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. Start by infusing the dried flowers in the carrier oil, placing them in a clean glass jar and leaving them in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in the double boiler, then slowly add the infused oil to the melted wax, stirring constantly to prevent lumps from forming. Continue heating the mixture until the wax and oil are fully incorporated, then remove it from the heat and let it cool and thicken. Once the cream has cooled and thickened, pour it into a clean container and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the cream, gently apply a small amount to the affected area around your eyes, such as the orbital bone, and massage it in gently.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for red eyes

Polyphenols are good for red eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and calm the irritated blood vessels in the eyes, soothing redness and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale cream is a natural remedy for red eyes. The plant, also known as comfrey, has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated skin and eyes. The cream works by reducing inflammation and swelling in the affected area. This is due to the presence of allantoin, a compound that helps to break down and remove dead skin cells and other debris that can cause redness and irritation. Allantoin also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the appearance of redness and puffiness. When applied to the eyes, the cream can help to calm and comfort the skin, reducing the appearance of redness and promoting a more even and healthy-looking complexion.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal cream that helps with red eyes, you'll need dried St. John's Wort flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, a beeswax base, and a cream base. First, steep the dried St. John's Wort flowers in the carrier oil for about 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture every day to help the herbs infuse into the oil. Then, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in the microwave in short intervals, stirring between each interval until it's fully melted. Once the beeswax is melted, slowly add the infused oil to the melted beeswax and stir well. Finally, add the cream base to the mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into a clean container, let it cool and solidify, and your Hypericum perforatum herbal cream is ready to use. To apply, gently pat a small amount under your eyes to help soothe and calm red, irritated eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams can be beneficial for red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. When we are tired or stressed, our eyes can become inflamed and turn red. Ginkgo biloba contains flavonoids and terpenoids that help to reduce swelling and ease the discomfort. The cream can be applied directly to the affected area to help calm the eyes and reduce redness. It also improves blood flow to the eyes, which can help to reduce the appearance of red veins and dark circles. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, Ginkgo biloba creams can provide quick relief for red, tired eyes. The natural ingredients in Ginkgo biloba creams are gentle on the skin and can be used regularly to help maintain healthy eyes. The cream can also be combined with other treatments to enhance its effectiveness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal cream for red eyes, start by drying the St John's Wort flowers, which are rich in hyperforin, a key compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes. Collect the flowers in a warm, dry place, away from direct sunlight, and store them in an airtight container once they are completely dry. Next, combine the dried flowers with a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, in a small saucepan, and heat the mixture over low heat for about 30 minutes, allowing the oil to infuse with the herbal properties. Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids, and then mix it with a thickening agent, like beeswax or shea butter, and a natural preservative, such as vitamin E oil, to create a smooth and stable cream. Finally, pour the cream into small jars and let it cool before use, applying a small amount to the affected eye area to help reduce redness and irritation.

Kaempferol is good for red eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the blood vessels in the eyes, which in turn can help to alleviate redness and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum cream is a natural remedy that can help with red eyes. The active ingredient, St. John's Wort, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. This can be caused by allergies, fatigue, or exposure to dust or chemicals. The cream works by soothing the eyelids and surrounding skin, calming the blood vessels and reducing their visibility. This can make red eyes appear less noticeable, giving you a more relaxed and refreshed appearance. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to prevent irritation and further inflammation, making it a good option for people who experience frequent or recurring red eyes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal cream that helps with red eyes, start by harvesting fresh St. John's Wort leaves and flowers in the summer months when they are in bloom. Clean and dry the leaves and flowers thoroughly, then chop them into small pieces. Combine 2 cups of the chopped herbs with 2 cups of carrier oil such as olive or jojoba oil in a clean glass jar. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days, to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Add 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to the infused oil and heat the mixture gently in a double boiler until the beeswax melts. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency suitable for applying to the skin. Pour the mixture into clean glass jars and let it cool completely before use. Once the cream is ready, apply it gently to the affected area around your eyes to help soothe and reduce redness.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for red eyes

intro_desc: Red eyes can be a real nuisance, causing discomfort and affecting your daily activities. One effective way to alleviate this issue is by using herbal creams containing soothing herbs like Calendula officinalis and Aloe barbadensis. Calendula, also known as marigold, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. It also has antimicrobial properties, which prevent infections and promote healing. Aloe, on the other hand, is known for its hydrating properties, which help calm and soothe irritated skin around the eyes.  Using herbal creams with these ingredients can help fix the issue of red eyes by providing a natural and gentle solution. When you have red eyes, it's not just about the physical discomfort - it can also affect your mood and daily routine. Imagine not being able to watch your favorite TV show or movie because your eyes are too sore to bear. Or, picture yourself feeling tired and irritable throughout the day because you're constantly rubbing your tired eyes. By addressing the root cause of red eyes, you can improve your quality of life and feel more energized and focused. By applying a soothing herbal cream, you can quickly alleviate the discomfort and get back to your daily activities with ease.
supplementary_best: To remedy red eyes, the best combination of herbal creams is Aloe barbadensis, Euphrasia officinalis, and Hypericum perforatum. Aloe barbadensis cream helps to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing inflammation and redness. Euphrasia officinalis cream is rich in antioxidants that help to protect the eyes from further damage and promote healing. Hypericum perforatum cream has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the eyes. When combined, these three creams work synergistically to provide effective relief from red eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of Hypericum perforatum cream complement the soothing properties of Aloe barbadensis cream, while the antioxidants in Euphrasia officinalis cream help to protect the eyes from further irritation. This combination helps to quickly and effectively reduce redness and inflammation, promoting a faster recovery and healthier eyes.
supplementary_avoid: If you have red eyes, it's best to avoid creams that contain herbs that can irritate or increase inflammation.  One such herb is 'Cinchona officinalis' (also known as 'Quinine'). This herb can be too harsh for sensitive eyes and may cause more redness and irritation.  Another herb to avoid is 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' (also known as 'Licorice root'). While it's often used to soothe skin, its active compound 'glycyrrhizin' can cause eye pressure and irritation, especially if you already have red eyes.  You should also avoid creams with 'Bacopa monnieri'. While it's commonly used to reduce eye strain, its side effects can include increased eye pressure and redness, especially if you have pre-existing eye problems.  'Capsicum annuum' (also known as 'Red pepper') can also irritate red eyes due to its spicy compound 'capsaicin'. This can further increase redness and discomfort.  Finally, 'Zanthoxylum bungeanum' (also known as 'Korean prickly ash') can be too harsh for sensitive eyes due to its high volatile oil content, which can cause eye irritation and redness.
supplementary_other_remedies: Cold compresses can be helpful in reducing redness in the eyes. A cold, wet cloth is placed over the eyes for a few minutes to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. This remedy works by narrowing the blood vessels and reducing the amount of blood that flows to the eyes, which in turn reduces the redness.  Aloe vera gel can also be applied around the eyes to help soothe and calm the skin. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and redness, making it a popular remedy for irritated eyes.  Cucumber slices can be placed over the eyes to provide relief from redness and puffiness. The cooling effect of the cucumber helps to reduce swelling and ease discomfort.  Epsom salt baths can be beneficial in reducing redness in the eyes. Soaking in a warm bath with Epsom salt can help to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort, which in turn can reduce redness in the eyes.  Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. Ginger tea or ginger compresses can be used to help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.  Warm washcloths can be used to help loosen and remove dirt and debris from the eyes, which can cause redness and irritation. The warmth of the washcloth can help to open up the eyes and allow the dirt to be easily removed, reducing redness and discomfort.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with red eyes often experience eye strain, headaches, and dryness due to prolonged exposure to screens or allergens. Some medicinal herbs that can help alleviate these symptoms include:  - **Eyebright** (Euphrasia officinalis): This herb is known to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. Its anti-inflammatory properties help soothe irritated eyes and ease eye strain.  - **Calendula** (Calendula officinalis): Calendula's anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties make it effective in treating eye infections and promoting healing in the eyes. It also helps to reduce redness and swelling.  - **Ginkgo biloba**: Ginkgo biloba is an herb that improves blood flow to the eyes, reducing inflammation and eye strain. Its antioxidant properties also help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals.  - **Eyewash herbs like Chamomile** (Matricaria chamomilla) and **Marshmallow** (Althaea officinalis): These herbs have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm irritated eyes and reduce redness. They can be used to make a soothing eyewash to provide quick relief.  - **Goldenseal** (Hydrastis canadensis): Goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. It also has antiseptic properties that can help to prevent eye infections.  - **Echinacea** (Echinacea spp.): Echinacea's immune-boosting properties can help to fight off eye infections and reduce inflammation in the eyes.  These herbs can be used in various forms, such as teas, tinctures, or eye washes, to provide relief from red eyes and associated symptoms.
